Data Analysis Assistant

  • Documented weekly trend findings in structured Microsoft Word reports submitted to content and marketing teams, translating data into clear briefs for non-technical audiences.
Microsoft Excel Microsoft Word Data Reporting Quantitative Analysis

MSc Dissertation (In Progress)

MSc Environmental Protection and Management, University of Edinburgh (Sep 2025 – Aug 2026)

Topic: GIS-Based Spatial Suitability Analysis for Onshore Wind Development Under Environmental and Planning Constraints in Scotland

Project Overview

Context — Scotland has set a national ambition of at least 20 GW installed onshore wind capacity by 2030, creating urgent demand for spatially informed site assessment that balances renewable energy targets with environmental protection and planning constraints.

Approach — Developing a GIS-based spatial suitability framework that integrates real-world government datasets, environmental constraint mapping (habitat sensitivity, peatland, biodiversity designations), and planning policy criteria to evaluate onshore wind site sustainability.

Key Methods — GIS spatial analysis, environmental constraint mapping, multi-criteria evaluation, data visualisation (maps and charts), use of official Scottish Government and environmental datasets.

Relevance — This project applies spatial analysis and policy interpretation to a real-world planning problem, with direct relevance to environmental impact assessment (EIA), site screening, and renewable energy roles.

GIS Data Visualisation Environmental Management

Habitat Restoration — Hermitage of Braid

Volunteering with The Conservation Volunteers (TCV), Edinburgh

Key Highlights

  • Constructed a bee bank for pollinator habitat — contributed to creating nesting sites for solitary bees as part of a site-wide biodiversity improvement programme.
  • Carried out habitat restoration tasks alongside experienced conservation practitioners, including vegetation clearance and soil preparation.
  • Gained direct experience of how conservation sites are managed under community and regulatory constraints, with ecological and land-use considerations.
Teamwork Physical Fieldwork Conservation Site Management

Drainage Maintenance — Blackford Hill

Volunteering with Lothians Conservation Volunteers (LCV), Edinburgh

Key Highlights

  • Cleared blocked drainage channels and removed overgrowth to improve water flow and reduce localised flooding risk on site.
  • Contributed to maintaining drainage infrastructure that supports both ecological function and community flood risk reduction.
  • Gained direct understanding of how drainage systems function within environmental and land management constraints, with relevance to infrastructure assessment and site appraisal.
Environmental Management Hydrological Assessment Teamwork
